pessimist.name: Walt Varga
pessimist.column:
Another El Niño loss, another wasted start. The Mariners’ offense continues to sputter like a gas-guzzling Buick in a rainstorm, and Jerry Dipoto’s Seattle is once again stuck in a loop of promises and platitudes. Yesterday’s 6-1 drubbing by the Astros wasn’t just another loss—it was a textbook case of why the front office’s winter moves feel like a game of Russian roulette. You promised a left-handed bat to fix this mess, and now it’s August, and the only thing arriving is a fresh wave of frustration.  

The offense’s quiet incompetence is a national disgrace. You’d think with all the money spent on free agents and prospects, there’d be a modicum of consistency at the plate. Instead, we’re treated to
